english: In the name of Allah the All-Merciful the Ever-Merciful
completion: Quran 1:1 begins with seeking Allah's name, mercy, and blessing before recitation and action.

arabic: ذَٰلِكَ الْكِتَابُ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ هُدًى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
english: This is the Book about which there is no doubt, a guidance for those mindful of Allah.
completion: Quran 2:2 describes the Qur'an as certain guidance for people of taqwa.

english: Islam is built upon five: testimony of faith, prayer, zakat, Hajj, and fasting Ramadan.
prompt: Which hadith mentions the five pillars?
completion: The five pillars are mentioned in the hadith of Ibn Umar, recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 8 and Sahih Muslim 16.

arabic: أَنْ تُؤْمِنَ بِاللَّهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الآخِرِ وَتُؤْمِنَ بِالْقَدَرِ خَيْرِهِ وَشَرِّهِ
english: Faith is to believe in Allah, His angels, His books, His messengers, the Last Day, and Divine Decree, its good and its evil.
prompt: Which hadith mentions the six articles of faith?
completion: The six articles of faith are mentioned in the Hadith of Jibril, recorded in Sahih Muslim 8.
